1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

12	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
13	
14	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
15	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
16	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
17	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
18	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
19	
20	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
21	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
22	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
23	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
24	
25	1. a binary tensor-product operation
26	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
27	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
28	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
29	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
30	
31	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
32	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
33	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
34	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
35	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
36	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
37	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
38

142	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
143	
144	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
145	
146	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
147	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
148	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
149	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
150	
151	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
152	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
153	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
154	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
155	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
156	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
157	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
158	
159	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
160	
161	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
162	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
163	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
164	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
165	
166	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
167	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
168	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
169	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
170	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
171	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
172	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
173	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
174	
175	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
176	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
177	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
178	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
179	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
180	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
181	law is definitional. -/
182	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
183	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
184	
185	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
186	
187	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
188	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
189	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
190	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
191	
192	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
193	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
194	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
195	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
196	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
197	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
198	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
199	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
200	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
201	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
202	
203	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
204	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
205	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
206	
207	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
208	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
209	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
210	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
211	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
212	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
213	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
214	
215	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
216	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
217	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
218	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
219	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
220	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
221	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
222	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
223	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
224	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
225	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
226	
227	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
228	
229	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
230	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
231	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
232	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
233	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
234	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
235	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
236	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
237	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
238	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
239	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
240	
241	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
242	
243	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
244	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
245	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
246	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
247	
248	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
249	
250	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
251	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
252	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
253	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
254	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
255	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
256	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
257	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
258	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
259	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
260	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
261	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
262	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
263	
264	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
265	sheaf.**
266	
267	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
268	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
269	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
270	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
271	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
272	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
273	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
274	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
275	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
276	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
277	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
278	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
279	
280	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
281	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
282	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
283	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
284	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
285	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
286	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
287	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
288	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
289	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
290	
291	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
292	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
293	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
294	sheafification counit. -/
295	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
296	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
297	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
298	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
299	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
300	
301	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
302	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
303	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
304	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
305	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
306	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
307	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
308	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
309	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
310	
311	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X`.** (Blueprint
312	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
313	For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
314	`(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
315	group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
316	
317	**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
318	hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
319	sectionwise flatness — false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
320	Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `η` the
321	sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
322	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso, giving
323	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
324	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`, `α` the
325	     presheaf-of-modules associator;
326	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso, giving
327	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
328	Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
329	`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (η = `toSheafify ∈ J.W`, and `J.W` is
330	stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
331	functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
332	`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
333	`letI instMS` below. -/
334	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
335	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
336	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
337	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
338	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
339	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
340	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
341	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
342	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
343	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
344	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
345	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
346	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
347	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
348	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
349	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
350	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
351	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
352	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
353	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
354	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
355	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
356	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
357	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
358	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
359	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
360	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
361	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
362	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
363	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
364	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
365	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
366	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
367	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
368	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
369	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
370	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
371	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
372	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
373	  have e2 := a.mapIso
374	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
375	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
376	
377	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
378	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
379	
380	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
381	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
382	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
383	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
384	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
385	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
386	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
387	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
388	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
389	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
390	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
391	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
392	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
393	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
394	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
395	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
396	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
397	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
398	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
399	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
400	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
401	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
402	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
403	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
404	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
405	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
406	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
407	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
408	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
409	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
410	
411	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
412	
413	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
414	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
415	
416	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
417	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
418	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
419	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
420	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
421	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
422	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
423	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
424	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
425	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
426	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
427	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
428	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
429	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
430	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
431	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
432	      definitionally.
433	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
434	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
435	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
436	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
437	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
438	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
439	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
440	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
441	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
442	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
443	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
444	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
445	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
446	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
447	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
448	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
449	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
450	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
451	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
452	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
453	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
454	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
455	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
456	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
457	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
458	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
459	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
460	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
461	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
462	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
463	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
464	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
465	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
466	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
467	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
468	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
469	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
470	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
471	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
472	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
473	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
474	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
475	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
476	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
477	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
478	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
479	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
480	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
481	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
482	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
483	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
484	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
485	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
486	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
487	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
488	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
489	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
490	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
491	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
492	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
493	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
494	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
495	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
496	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
497	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
498	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
499	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
500	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
501	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
502	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
503	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
504	    intro U
505	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
506	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
507	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
508	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
509	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
510	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
511	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
512	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
513	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
514	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
515	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
516	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm
517	
518	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
519	
520	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
521	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
522	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
523	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
524	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
525	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
526	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
527	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
528	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
529	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
530	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
531	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
532	  intro x
533	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
534	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
535	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
536	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
537	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
538	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
539	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
540	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
541	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
542	    tensorObj_unit_iso
543	
544	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
545	
546	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
547	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
548	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
549	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
550	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
551	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
552	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
553	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
554	
555	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
556	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
557	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
558	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
559	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
560	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
561	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
562	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
563	    IsIso φ := by
564	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
565	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
566	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
567	    intro x
568	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
569	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
570	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
571	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
572	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
573	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).

594	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
595	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
596	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
597	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
598	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
599	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
600	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
601	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
602	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
603	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
604	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
605	    M ⟶ N :=
606	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
607	
608	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
609	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
610	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
611	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
612	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
613	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
614	  rfl

710	/-! ## §5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`
711	
712	This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `⊗`-invertible
713	`𝒪_X`-modules (blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
714	existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no

717	
718	/-- **Step 1 — associator on `⊗`-invertible objects** (blueprint
719	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
720	*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
721	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
722	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
723	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
724	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
725	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
726	
727	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
728	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
729	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
730	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
731	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
732	    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
733	      ≅ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
734	  tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
735	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
736	      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
737	        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) ≪≫
738	        tensorObj_assoc_iso) ≪≫
739	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm
740	
741	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
742	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
743	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
744	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
745	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
746	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
747	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
748	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
749	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
750	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
751	
752	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
753	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
754	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
755	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
756	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
757	
758	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
759	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
760	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
761	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
762	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
763	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
764	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
765	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
766	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
767	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
768	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
769	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩

771	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
772	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
773	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
774	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
775	  iseqv :=
776	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩

843	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
844	
845	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
846	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme

852	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**
853	
854	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
855	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons
856	of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
857	the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
858	concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
859	hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
860	genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
861	adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
862	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
863	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
864	      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
865	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
866	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
867	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
868	  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
869	  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
870	  intro U
871	  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
872	  apply LinearMap.ext
873	  intro a
874	  rfl

876	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
877	(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**
878	
879	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
880	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
881	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
882	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.
883	
884	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
885	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
886	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
887	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
888	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
889	and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
890	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
891	`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.
892	
893	Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
894	`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for

1101	Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`

1103	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
1104	topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
1105	`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
1106	`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.
1107	
1108	The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,
1109	the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
1110	middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂` (the form

1115	that an eventual oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.

1120	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1121	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1122	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
1123	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1124	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1125	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
1126	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance

1194	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso

1196	        (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M)

1198	/-- **The sheaf-level pullback–tensor comparison map `δ_sheaf`** (blueprint
1199	`lem:pullback_tensor_map`). For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and arbitrary
1200	`M N : X.Modules`, the canonical comparison morphism
1201	`f^*(M ⊗_X N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗_Y f^*N`. It is the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf-level
1202	oplax comparison `δ` (`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) through sheafification, assembled
1203	from the `sheafificationCompPullback` device, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`, and `pullbackValIso`.
1204	This is a *map only*: in general it is not asserted to be an isomorphism (the invertible
1205	case is upgraded to an iso by local trivialisation in `IsInvertible.pullback`). -/
1206	noncomputable def pullbackTensorMap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1207	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ⟶
1208	      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
1209	  let φ := f.toRingCatSheafHom
1210	  let φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1211	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := φ.hom
1212	  let a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
1213	  refine ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback φ).app
1214	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1215	  refine a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val) ≫ ?_
1216	  refine (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
1217	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj M.val)
1218	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1219	  exact a_Y.map (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1220	    (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1221	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1222	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))

1224	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1225	
1226	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1227	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1228	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1229	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1230	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1231	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1232	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1233	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1234	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1235	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1236	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1237	
1238	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1239	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1240	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1241	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1242	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1243	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1244	
1245	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1246	
1247	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1248	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1249	
1250	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1251	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1252	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1253	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1254	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1255	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1256	
1257	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1258	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1259	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1260	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1261	  inferInstanceAs
1262	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1263	
1264	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1265	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1266	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1267	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1268	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1269	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1270	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1271	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1272	
1273	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1274	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1275	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1276	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1277	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1278	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1279	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1280	
1281	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1282	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1283	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1284	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1285	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1286	
1287	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1288	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1289	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1290	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1291	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1292	
1293	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1294	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1295	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1296	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1297	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1298	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1299	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1300	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1301	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1302	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1303	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1304	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1305	
1306	end PullbackLanDecomposition
1307	
1308	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1309

1313	
1314	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1315

1320	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1321	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1322	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1323	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1324	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1325	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
